rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by ensuring all identifiers in AGREE contracts are visible in the current AADL scope and properly declared.
Use only valid AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts; avoid referencing undefined or package-qualified identifiers.
Avoid placing guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings and ensure proper contract structure.
generic_rules: When generating AGREE contracts, validate that all referenced identifiers exist in the current scope and are properly typed.
Ensure AGREE scalar type declarations match AADL conventions (int, real, bool) to prevent resolution errors.
Prefer minimal repairs that address root causes of diagnostics without introducing new architecture elements.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks and placed in appropriate component type or implementation scopes.
Assignments in implementation scope should reference existing output features or declared auxiliary variables.
Helper declarations (const, eq) must reference valid identifiers and have correct types to avoid 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors.
anti_patterns: Do not introduce package-qualified identifiers or types that are not present in the AADL model or its dependencies.
Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope; use assign for output bindings instead.
Do not add new architecture elements when fixing AGREE diagnostics; focus on correcting existing references and types.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about annex placement
